title: NOC 21112 – Agricultural Representatives, Consultants & Specialists | GTR Immigration
description: Agricultural Representatives, Consultants, and Specialists play a critical role in crop productivity, livestock management, sustainability, and farm-business optimization across Canada. Classified under NOC 21112 (TEER 1), this profession is in long-term national demand—creating strong pathways for skilled immigrants through Express Entry–linked streams, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and employer-supported work permits.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Advising farmers on crops, fertilizers, livestock, and disease prevention
Researching agricultural methods and preparing reports
Liaising with researchers, educators, and government agencies
Using drones for crop analysis, mapping, and spraying
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in agriculture or a related science
Eligibility for membership in a provincial institute of agrology
Mandatory membership in Quebec’s professional agronomist order
job_outlook: Between 2022–2031, Canada is expected to create 11,500 new positions, while about 9,600 workers will enter the labour market—indicating favourable immigration prospects for internationally trained agricultural specialists.
additional_information: Demand for agricultural specialists continues to grow as Canada invests in food security, climate-resilient farming, and agri-technology. Employers typically include:
Provincial and federal agriculture departmentsFarm-support agencies and co-operativesCrop-input companiesResearch institutionsAgribusiness consultanciesSelf-employed advisors
Annual salaries commonly range from $47,100–$97,200 CAD, depending on experience, province, and professional licensing.
Why Choose Canada in This Occupation?

Nationwide shortages in rural regionsPR-linked skilled-worker immigration programsStable public-sector demandCompetitive wagesFree public healthcareFamily sponsorship optionsStrong sustainability policies
